references.title: Student’s performance prediction model and affecting factors using classification techniques

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Trend of GPA from Previous Semester
extracted.Data sources: Questionnaires, - Student characteristics and Academic History.
extracted.Features: [Gender, Living Location, Stay, SSC Perrcentage, HSSC Percentage, Interest in Higher Study, Preferred TEaching Medium, Mobile Type, University Transport, Previous Semesters’ GPAs, Study Program, Family Status, Family Income, Father Qualification,  Mother Qualification, Father Occupation, Mother Occupation, Friend Behavior, Preferred Teaching Aids, Departments Support, Computer Lab Facilities, Tools Availability in Labs, Internet Facility at home, Preferred Teaching Method, Time Spent on Special Network, Time for Mobile Games, Time for Physical Games, Type of Physiscal Games]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Missing values were filled using kNN imputation… 

Class labels were also generated from the Linear Fitting of the learning of the student.
extracted.Comments:
The authors test 3 distinct separations when it comes to the holdout method - 70-30, 80-20, 90-10. Published results refer to resuults on the test data.

The authors compare 2 distinct algorithms with 3 distinct separations. 

Work finishes with interpretation of the features and discussed how these could/would lead to a specific level of performance.
extracted.Limitations:
Holdout Method

Small sample size

Limited Number of Algorithms

Data collection comes from Self-Reports

annotations.text: Students are one of the primary sources of data for educational contexts and it is in the benefit of theses institutions to use this data to identify students who may require special attention. The paper presents an hybrid of DT with SVM model for student performance.

Abstract does not explicitly mention the metrics used for evaluation but it does seem that DT was better (possible typo). Moreover, the authors have also identified 7 possible factors that affect student performance.
